title: Mastering VS Code Copilot by Understanding Its Context Window
author: Elye
text:
Member-only story
Learning AI Coding
Mastering VS Code Copilot by Understanding Its Context Window
Expanding Your AI Coding Skills by Understanding How VS Code Copilot Works Under the Hood and How Its Context Window Is Structured
If you’ve enjoyed building software with AI and are amazed by what agentic coding can do, you may still wonder how to get the most out of it — while keeping token costs under control. Understanding what’s happening under the hood makes a big difference. In this article, I break down how VS Code Copilot actually works behind the scenes.
The Basic of LLM for Agentic Coding
The real engine behind agentic AI coding is the LLM (Large Language Model). At its core, an LLM is software trained to predict and generate tokens in a way that aligns with real-world expectations, based on patterns learned from its training data.
On its own, an LLM is little more than a sophisticated “chat” machine. But when its outputs are treated as instructions for tools — such as reading, editing, or writing files, or running…
